rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF JOHN J. CURTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #17, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. appearance required. remote appearance encouraged. please review the general court order for more information. Issues: 1) The petition on page 3 lines 20-24 states Konstantine Demiris is requesting fees for their successful petition for appointment as successors conservators of the person. Conservator's were appointed 05/28/15. The timesheets are dated 9/11/17 to 03/25/19. 2) Petitioner is requesting guideline fees of 6% of receipts $3,685.91. The original timesheets show $3,210.00. The Verified Supplement timesheets show $3,220.00.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ELIZABETH EMMA CAULFIELD MAYO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/20/2020 to allow the assigned court investigator to complete a report. The following issues must be resolved: 1) No proposed orders have been provided. Proposed orders must be one-sided hard copies mailed to Room 202 or placed in a drop box outside Room 103 and may not be e-filed, faxed, or e-mailed. 2) The date of issuance of letters set forth in paragraph 2 of the petition is incorrect. 3) An additional bond of $69,000 must be filed before the subject account can be approved. See LRSF 14.40(A) & CRC 7.204. 4) Allegations regarding the application of Probate Code § 1064(a)(1)-(3) are needed. 5) What is the anticipated date of the conservatee's move to San Mateo County? 6) Was the $495 payment of legal fees to Hall Law Firm on 04/01/2018 made pursuant to a prior court order? 7) The bank letters attached to the petition as Exhibit B are copies. Original bank letters must be filed. See LRSF 14.62. 8) According to the bank letters, one of the accounts (ending in 6768) appears to be titled under the trust. Why is the petitioner holding title to this account as trustee, not as conservator? See CRC 7.1059(b)(7). 9) According to the bank letters, the ending market value of DBX ETF TR XTRACK MSCI EAFE is $108,367.23, while Schedule E shows that the value is $108,368.23. Why are they different? 10) The proof of service shows a different address for Alexander Caulfield Mayo. Has he moved from San Francisco to Mill Valley? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CHARLES P. WAITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2020. The following issues must be resolved: 1) The inventory and appraisal does not state the date of appointment. 2) No account statement showing the balance immediately preceding the date of the petitioner's appointment is on file. See Probate Code § 2620(c)(2). 3) The current bond ($10,000) is insufficient and must be increased. 4) Was notice of the second amendment given to Brian Long, co-conservator of the conservatee's spouse? See CRC 7.51(c)(1). 4) The proofs of service of the first and second amendments show an incorrect zip code for the conservatee's spouse. Was she properly served? 5) Does the cash inventoried as the sole conservatorship asset represent a return of the $500,000 funds transferred from the trust of the conservatee and his spouse? 6) Were some of the services described in the fee declarations of the petitioner and her attorneys rendered for the benefit of the trust? 7) Have the petitioner's attorneys received any compensation from the trust for their services rendered in the related trust case? 8) The small estate affidavit has the following defects: A) The original affidavit with a certified copy of the death certificate must be filed. See LRSF 14.78(J)(5) & 14.93(E)(2). B) Paragraph 5 of the affidavit identifies the Wells Fargo Bank account, but this account is not listed as property on hand at the end of the supplemental account period. C) The statement in paragraph 6 of the affidavit must be corrected, as the successor is the trust, and the declarants are acting on behalf of the successor trust. See Probate Code §§ 13006(a), 13051(b), & 13101(a)(8)(B). *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GIULIANA MAUBERRET DECLARATION OF TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Property As Assets Of The Trust And Instructing The Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/20/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issue must be filed: According to the judgment settling the estate of the trustor's predeceased spouse, the trustor was only given a life estate interest in the subject real property. The judgment requires the real property to be distributed to the decedent's children upon the trustor's death. Did the trustor have any authority to transfer the real property to her trust? Is a Heggstad petition appropriate with respect to the real property in light of the judgment? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing. close of estate set for 9/28/21

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTYE M. MCNAULTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 12/27/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TOKIE MURAKAMI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to july 27, 2020. counsel should note that when working remotely, many examiners do not have access to a fax machine, so providing an email address on the pleadings is important in order to avoid delays. - no later than noon on 7/20/20, counsel must file (not just upload!) a declaration signed and verified by petitioner that provides the address of the real property and states what has been happening with that property for the last 7 years - is it occupied, who is paying the expenses? collecting rent? etc.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALMA TINA HOGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (Telephonic appearance encouraged.)
